pnl.title: P&L
pnl.subtitle: Monthly profit & expense analysis
pnl.description: Model your monthly income and expense report. Separate controllable and fixed costs, then track net profit.

insights.foodCostHigh: Food cost is above the industry benchmark ({value} target exceeded)
insights.laborCostHigh: Labor cost is excessive ({value} target exceeded)

education.whatBody: Profit & Loss is the financial statement that shows how much a restaurant earns and spends during a period. It should be prepared every month.

knowledge.title: Understand
knowledge.subtitle: Know the logic behind the numbers and take control.
knowledge.controllableBody: Food, labor, ads, promos, utilities, and repairs can be changed. A large share of P&L control is in your hands.
knowledge.uncontrollableBody: Rent, tax, insurance, and depreciation are more fixed. Focus first on expenses you can influence.
knowledge.primeCostBody: The two largest restaurant costs are food and labor. Prime Cost is the most critical P&L metric. Keep it below 65% of sales.

warnings.primeCost: Prime cost above 70% means the restaurant is near the loss zone.
warnings.lowNet: Net profit below 5% is risky; one mistake can turn the month negative.
warnings.highRent: Rent above 12% may mean the location is too expensive.
warnings.strongNet: Net profit above 15% signals strong performance and room to invest.

advice.title: DK Agency advice
advice.body: A restaurant that prepares P&L once a month is already ahead of most competitors. Real power comes from weekly tracking because trends appear earlier.

ocaq.title: OCAQ Panel
ocaq.body: Automatic P&L reports, food cost tracking, and labor cost analysis. Everything in one panel.

ahilik.quote1: "Business without ledger, earnings without blessing." — Akhi notebook
ahilik.quote2: "The master must know his craft, the apprentice his notebook."

foodCost.title: The Hard Truth About Food Cost

staffRetention.title: Staff Retention
staffRetention.subtitle: See your team turnover in numbers. Calculate the turnover rate, replacement cost per employee, and annual impact on your bottom line.
staffRetention.calculatorSubtitle: Calculate staff turnover based on salary and team size.
staffRetention.turnoverRateBenchmark: The global restaurant average is 60-80%. In the Baku market, some venues reach 100-150%.
staffRetention.replacementCostNote: Industry guidelines suggest replacing one employee costs approximately 2-3 months of salary.
staffRetention.annualLossNote: This figure excludes training losses, order errors, and service quality decline. Real losses are higher.
staffRetention.preShiftTitle: Why does the pre-shift meeting work?
staffRetention.preShiftBody: A 10-minute team huddle before each shift reduces surprises and stress, aligning everyone on VIP reservations, daily specials, and yesterday's issues. It is one of the cheapest yet highest-ROI staffing tools in Baku restaurants.
staffRetention.dkAdviceBody: If turnover exceeds 80%, the problem is systemic, not just pay. Without documented hiring, onboarding, pre-shift and exit interview processes, building a stable team is very difficult.
staffRetention.reason4: Dissatisfaction with salary and tip system
staffRetention.strategy1: Hire for attitude, teach skills later.
staffRetention.strategy2: Create an onboarding, mentoring and cross-training plan for the first 2 weeks.
staffRetention.strategy3: Offer staff meals, flexible scheduling and a recognition program in addition to salary.
staffRetention.strategy4: Hold a 10-minute pre-shift meeting before every shift.
staffRetention.strategy5: Share tip policies in writing and transparently.
staffRetention.strategy6: Show career paths and salary tiers upfront.
staffRetention.strategy7: Conduct exit interviews with every departing employee and look for patterns.

branding.title: Branding
branding.subtitle: Go beyond your logo. Build your brand statement, visual identity and social media strategy step by step like a workbook.
branding.workbookSubtitle: Define your brand statement with a 12-item checklist.
branding.dkAdviceBody: Write your brand statement first, not your logo. Going straight to design before defining your target audience, service tone and menu language leads to expensive rework 2-3 times over.
branding.checklistStrategyItem1: Write your restaurant's one-sentence brand statement.
branding.checklistStrategyItem2: Define the age, income and lifestyle profile of your target audience.
branding.checklistStrategyItem3: Write why your brand exists in simple language for your team.
branding.checklistVisualItem1: Prepare color and black-and-white versions of your logo.
branding.checklistVisualItem2: Define a palette of maximum 3-4 colors.
branding.checklistVisualItem3: Choose a maximum of 2 fonts and use them consistently across all materials.
branding.checklistExperienceItem1: Align menu design with the brand feel.
branding.checklistExperienceItem2: Define a single visual style for food photography.
branding.checklistExperienceItem3: Train staff on brand language and service tone.
branding.checklistGrowthItem3: Build a word-of-mouth plan with referrals, VIP perks and surprise moments.
branding.brandLevel1Desc: Logo, color, font, menu, interior and packaging.
branding.brandLevel2Desc: How you speak on the phone, in the menu and on social media.
branding.brandLevel3Desc: What your team makes customers feel and what they protect.
branding.brandPromise1: The freshest seafood in Baku, in a calm family setting.
branding.brandPromise2: Fast, affordable, filling lunch — perfect for a workday.
branding.brandPromise3: A modern take on Azerbaijani cuisine — for special evenings.
branding.social1: Instagram: build brand recognition through visual and atmosphere content.
branding.social2: TikTok: grow reach with short kitchen and service videos.
branding.social3: Google Business: don't neglect search, maps and review management.

basabas.title: Break-even
basabas.subtitle: 3 key numbers: monthly minimum revenue, daily customer target and safety margin.
basabas.educationSubtitle: Running a restaurant without knowing your revenue floor is a risky game.
basabas.whatBody: The break-even point is the minimum revenue at which a restaurant makes neither profit nor loss. Every month below this figure is a loss.
basabas.fixedVsVariableSubtitle: Fixed costs don't change even when sales do.
basabas.fixedCostsDesc: Rent, salaries, insurance, loan payments, licenses. These are paid even when sales are zero.
basabas.variableCostsDesc: Food, beverages, packaging, delivery. These grow as sales grow.
basabas.contributionMarginDesc: What remains after subtracting variable costs from sales goes toward covering fixed costs.
basabas.safetyMarginBody: Shows how far current sales are above break-even. If below 20%, you are in the risk zone.
basabas.dkAdviceBody: Track break-even weekly, not just monthly. Problems usually start mid-month, not at month's end.
basabas.ocaqBody: Automatically track break-even revenue, daily customer targets and sales gaps in one dashboard.
basabas.article3Title: The Bloody Truth About Food Cost

toolkit.pnlDesc: Create monthly income-expense reports, find the break-even point.
toolkit.checklistDesc: 43-item complete checklist — legal, venue, menu, staffing, marketing.

sezonAnalitikasi.title: Season Analytics
sezonAnalitikasi.subtitle: A 12-month cash-flow, labor budget, and inventory forecast calibrated for Azerbaijan holidays, resort seasons, and Ramadan behavior.

monthlyRevenue.label: Average monthly revenue
monthlyRevenue.validation: Revenue must be a number greater than 0.

laborPercent.label: Target labor cost percent
laborPercent.validation: Labor cost percent must be between 15 and 45.

foodCostPercent.label: Target food cost percent
foodCostPercent.validation: Food cost percent must be between 20 and 45.

sezonAnalitikasi.ramadanNote: During the Ramadan window, daytime traffic may drop about 20%, while iftar evenings may rise about 35%. Monthly totals can stay near neutral, but staff and inventory should be planned by hour.
sezonAnalitikasi.deadMonthWarning: Dead month: reduce advance purchasing, shorten shifts, and prepare a local campaign.
sezonAnalitikasi.ahilikQuote: Ahilik wisdom: a master builds reserves in abundant months and keeps order in narrow months.
sezonAnalitikasi.tier_required_body: Season Analytics belongs to KALFA because it combines cash-flow forecasting with labor and inventory planning.

reklamRoi.title: Ad ROI
reklamRoi.subtitle: Turn awareness and conversion campaigns from vanity metrics into ROAS, CAC, and LTV:CAC.

campaignType.title: Campaign type
campaignType.awarenessDescription: Measured by reach, CPM, and estimated EMV; not interpreted as direct sales.
campaignType.conversionDescription: Measured by ROAS, CAC, conversion output, and LTV:CAC health.

averageOrderValue.label: Average order value
averageOrderValue.validation: Average order value must be greater than 0.

repeatPurchasePercent.label: Repeat purchase percent
repeatPurchasePercent.validation: Repeat purchase must be between 0 and 95.

validation.channelBudget: Enter budget for at least one channel.

results.ltvFormulaNote: LTV is a simple estimate: AOV × (1 / (1 − repeat purchase percent)).

benchmarkNote.title: Benchmark note
benchmarkNote.body: Global influencer marketing return is often cited around $5.78 per $1; treat it as a comparison signal, not a hard target. A healthy LTV:CAC target is ≥ 3:1.

reklamRoi.awarenessInfo: This campaign is not measured as a sales campaign. In awareness mode, reach, CPM, and estimated EMV matter more than ROAS.
reklamRoi.ahilikQuote: Ahilik wisdom: a master follows profit and trust, not noise.
reklamRoi.tier_required_body: Ad ROI is in KALFA because it calculates channel ROAS, CAC, and LTV:CAC.

sosialMetrik.title: Social Media Metrics Analyzer
sosialMetrik.subtitle: Compare your Instagram and TikTok engagement rate with HoReCa benchmarks
sosialMetrik.tier_required_body: Social Metrics Analyzer includes sector benchmarks and content strategy advice, so it belongs to the KALFA tier.

inputs.reach_placeholder: Provides more accurate results if available

actions.title: Action recommendations
actions.switch_to_video_detail: Reels achieve 2-5x higher ER on average — prioritize the shift from static posts to video format.
actions.reply_fast_detail: Comments answered within the first hour boost future post ER by ~23% (algorithm signal).
actions.save_content_detail: Recipes, checklists, infographics — followers who save your content get shown more of it.
actions.keep_going_detail: You're above the benchmark. Maintain consistency and keep experimenting.
actions.experiment_detail: Story-driven carousels, behind-the-scenes Reels — different formats attract new audiences.
actions.ugc_detail: Customer photos/videos look more authentic, build trust, and boost shares.

infoLowEr.title: Why does your ER seem low?
infoLowEr.body: Between 2024-2026, average Instagram ER has declined across all sectors. This isn't your fault — it's due to algorithm changes and platform saturation. What matters is your position relative to sector benchmarks.

infoMethodology.title: ER methodology
infoMethodology.body: This tool uses follower-based ER: (likes+comments+saves+shares) / (posts×followers) × 100. Reach-based ER typically appears higher. Differences between sources are normal — stick to one methodology.

restoranAudit.title: Restaurant Audit
restoranAudit.subtitle: A 30-question management mirror for small restaurants: cash, costs, kitchen, service, digital presence, and compliance risks.
restoranAudit.tier_required_body: Restaurant Audit is included in the PRO plan. It turns cash, prime cost, kitchen, and compliance gaps into an action plan.

finance_cash.name: Finance & cash
finance_cash.description: Cash closing, fiscal discipline, monthly cost and margin visibility.

operations_kitchen.name: Operations & kitchen
operations_kitchen.description: Food safety, inventory, waste, FIFO and recipe control.

staff_service.name: Staff & service
staff_service.description: Training, service protocol, upselling, shifts and staff stability.

customer_experience.name: Customer experience
customer_experience.description: Reviews, complaints, loyalty, table turnover and return rate.

digital_presence.name: Digital presence
digital_presence.description: Google Business, online orders, mobile menu and social activity.

compliance_risk.name: Compliance & risk
compliance_risk.description: Food safety registration, medical checks, temperature, disinfection and labor risk.

finance_cash_q1.text: Is daily cash closing reconciled between POS/Z-report and actual cash/card totals?
finance_cash_q1.tooltip: If end-of-day gaps are not visible, losses, errors and cancellations escape control.

finance_cash_q2.text: Is every sale closed with a fiscal receipt and are discounts, cancellations and refunds tracked separately?
finance_cash_q2.tooltip: Fiscal discipline keeps a real sales trail and strengthens internal control.

finance_cash_q3.text: Does the monthly expense report show sales, food, labor, rent, utilities, marketing and net profit together?
finance_cash_q3.tooltip: Separate notes are not enough for decisions; the owner needs one-page visibility.

finance_cash_q4.text: Is prime cost, food plus labor percentage, tracked monthly and is actual food cost compared with recipe cost?
finance_cash_q4.tooltip: Food and labor are the main margin signal. A small drift can create a large annual loss.

finance_cash_q5.text: Are the weekly top-10 sold products known and is each product margin calculated?
finance_cash_q5.tooltip: A high-selling item with weak margin is a hidden loss source on the menu.

operations_kitchen_q1.text: Are HACCP and food safety practices part of daily kitchen operations?
operations_kitchen_q1.tooltip: The procedure must work during prep and service, not only on paper.

operations_kitchen_q2.text: Is inventory counted periodically and compared with system stock?
operations_kitchen_q2.tooltip: Without counts, theft, waste and purchasing errors stay invisible.

operations_kitchen_q3.text: Are waste and loss recorded in a separate log?
operations_kitchen_q3.tooltip: If discarded product is not explained, rising food cost becomes hard to diagnose.

operations_kitchen_q4.text: Do FIFO and date labels work across all prep and storage areas?
operations_kitchen_q4.tooltip: Undated product is a quality, compliance and cost risk.

operations_kitchen_q5.text: Is the gap between actual food cost and theoretical recipe food cost tracked?
operations_kitchen_q5.tooltip: Portion drift, waste and price changes show up in this gap.

staff_service_q1.text: Is there written onboarding material and a checklist for new staff?
staff_service_q1.tooltip: When training stays verbal, service standards change from shift to shift.

staff_service_q2.text: Is the service protocol applied consistently by the team?
staff_service_q2.tooltip: Greeting, order taking, complaint handling and payment should follow the same rhythm.

staff_service_q3.text: Does the team know specific products and phrases for upselling?
staff_service_q3.tooltip: Upselling is a system for proposing higher-margin items, not random pressure.

staff_service_q4.text: Are shifts planned around peak and quiet hours?
staff_service_q4.tooltip: Wrong shifts damage both service speed and labor percentage.

staff_service_q5.text: Is staff turnover and exit reason tracked monthly?
staff_service_q5.tooltip: Repeated exit reasons are an early warning signal for management.

customer_experience_q1.text: Are Google and social reviews monitored weekly?
customer_experience_q1.tooltip: Review flow is the real market mirror for the restaurant.

customer_experience_q2.text: Is there a process for complaint response, correction and follow-up?
customer_experience_q2.tooltip: A complaint is not just a reply; it is a process to prevent repeated errors.

customer_experience_q3.text: Is there a loyalty or return-visit mechanism in use?
customer_experience_q3.tooltip: New customers are expensive; returning customers protect margin.

customer_experience_q4.text: Are table turnover and waiting time tracked?
customer_experience_q4.tooltip: During peak hours, table turnover directly changes sales capacity.

customer_experience_q5.text: Is repeat customer percentage and reason for return known?
customer_experience_q5.tooltip: Loyalty should be measured behavior, not a feeling.

digital_presence_q1.text: Is the Google Business profile current with photos, hours and menu information?
digital_presence_q1.tooltip: Search results often create the first decision to visit.

digital_presence_q2.text: Is the online order or reservation process clear?
digital_presence_q2.tooltip: Extra steps in the customer journey create sales loss.

digital_presence_q3.text: Are mobile menu, prices and photo quality comfortable for the customer?
digital_presence_q3.tooltip: Mobile viewing is one of the core restaurant selection moments.

digital_presence_q4.text: Is social activity connected to campaign and menu priorities?
digital_presence_q4.tooltip: Posting is not enough; content should support sales and reputation goals.

digital_presence_q5.text: Is campaign source tracked through promo code, UTM or channel notes?
digital_presence_q5.tooltip: If source is unknown, working channels cannot be separated from weak ones.

compliance_risk_q1.text: Are food safety registration and sanitary documents in order?
compliance_risk_q1.tooltip: Compliance should be tracked as a working checklist, without relying on memory.

compliance_risk_q2.text: Are medical checks for food-contact staff tracked?
compliance_risk_q2.tooltip: Medical monitoring reduces both compliance and customer safety risk.

compliance_risk_q3.text: If delivery exists, is the temperature regime maintained?
compliance_risk_q3.tooltip: Temperature drift during delivery is a quality and safety risk.

compliance_risk_q4.text: Are cleaning, disinfection and pest-control actions logged?
compliance_risk_q4.tooltip: A process without logs is hard to prove in audit and hard to manage internally.

compliance_risk_q5.text: Are employment contracts, insurance and safety processes in order?
compliance_risk_q5.tooltip: Team risk directly affects the restaurant legal and operational stability.

sagird.name: Apprentice - critical attention
sagird.description: Core control gaps exist; close red risks first.

kalfa.name: Journeyman - developing
kalfa.description: The base works, but systems and measurement must close weak spots.

usta.name: Master level
usta.description: Core processes are measured and managed; optimization comes next.

finance_cash.critical: Build a 7-day cash closing and monthly cost report in one table first.
finance_cash.warning: Move prime cost and top product margin into a weekly review rhythm.
finance_cash.stable: Finance rhythm is stable; now deepen margin optimization.

operations_kitchen.critical: Make FIFO, date labels and waste log mandatory daily processes this week.
operations_kitchen.warning: Connect inventory count with actual-vs-recipe variance in the monthly report.
operations_kitchen.stable: Kitchen control works; now split waste reasons in more detail.

staff_service.critical: Write the training checklist and service protocol, then assign it to the shift lead.
staff_service.warning: Review upselling phrases and peak-hour staffing every week.
staff_service.stable: Team rhythm is stable; now measure sales and service quality.

customer_experience.critical: Standardize complaint response and weekly review monitoring immediately.
customer_experience.warning: Track table turnover and return visits in a simple report.
customer_experience.stable: Customer process works; now segment loyalty reasons.

digital_presence.critical: Update Google Business, mobile menu and order path this week.
digital_presence.warning: Start tracking campaign source with promo code and channel notes.
digital_presence.stable: Digital base is stable; now optimize channel ROI.

compliance_risk.critical: Collect documents, medical checks, temperature and disinfection logs in one compliance folder.
compliance_risk.warning: Attach the compliance checklist to a monthly manager review.
compliance_risk.stable: Risk control works; now check continuity of proof logs.

primeCostWarning.title: Prime cost is not tracked
primeCostWarning.body: If food and labor percentages are invisible, a small drift can become a large annual loss.

unknown.title: What do you not know?
unknown.body: Running a restaurant without these critical numbers and documents is blind management.
unknown.empty: No full missing answer on critical management questions.

restoranAudit.sheddNote: This is an internal self-assessment; SHEDD certification is a separate process.
restoranAudit.ahilikQuote: In the craft tradition, work that is not measured cannot be improved; measured work grows with mastery.

trendAnaliz.title: Trend Analysis
trendAnaliz.subtitle: Rank 2026 HoReCa trends for your restaurant profile and see the first practical step for the top 3.
trendAnaliz.tier_required_body: Trend Analysis is included in the PRO plan. It prioritizes 2026 HoReCa trends for your profile.

value_pricing.title: Value and price sensitivity
value_pricing.description: In 2026 guests want clear value, not just a low price.
value_pricing.azContext: For small restaurants, a value lunch set, clear portion message and simple bundle are easier than a full repositioning.
value_pricing.fallbackFirstStep: Create one value set from 3 best sellers and highlight it separately on the menu.

delivery_first.title: Delivery-first format
delivery_first.description: Delivery and takeaway menus need separate thinking around packaging, speed and margin.
delivery_first.azContext: WhatsApp ordering, a simple delivery zone and travel-safe menu items are realistic first steps.
delivery_first.fallbackFirstStep: Pick 5 items that travel well and turn them into a separate WhatsApp order list.

ai_ordering.title: AI and digital ordering
ai_ordering.description: Digital ordering, personalization and repeat offers are moving into daily restaurant operations.
ai_ordering.azContext: Before expensive software, improve Google Business, Instagram DM and WhatsApp reply templates.
ai_ordering.fallbackFirstStep: Write 5 WhatsApp quick replies and 3 repeat-order offers for the team phone.

functional_health.title: Health and functional menu
functional_health.description: Guests are looking for lighter portions, allergen clarity and functional choices.
functional_health.azContext: A small restaurant can start with two lighter plates and simple allergen clarity instead of rebuilding the menu.
functional_health.fallbackFirstStep: Mark 2 lighter dishes and show ingredient or allergen clarity with a simple icon.

nostalgia_escape.title: Nostalgia and flavor escape
nostalgia_escape.description: Comfort food, familiar taste and story-led presentation answer the guest need for reassurance.
nostalgia_escape.azContext: Modernizing a national flavor, family recipe or seasonal comfort dish works well in the local market.
nostalgia_escape.fallbackFirstStep: Write a short story for one signature dish and train the team to present it in the same sentence.

beverage_focus.title: Beverage focus and margin
beverage_focus.description: Cold, caffeinated, photogenic and functional drinks are strong for both content and margin.
beverage_focus.azContext: Homemade lemonade, cold coffee and a seasonal mocktail are low-risk tests for cafes and coastal restaurants.
beverage_focus.fallbackFirstStep: Add one cold coffee, one homemade lemonade and one photogenic seasonal drink to a mini drink board.

sustainability_local.title: Sustainability and local sourcing
sustainability_local.description: Guests respond to less waste, local sourcing and responsible menu stories.
sustainability_local.azContext: Small restaurants do not need big ESG claims; local product names, waste logging and seasonal messages are enough.
sustainability_local.fallbackFirstStep: Name one local supplier or seasonal ingredient on the menu and start a daily waste note.

experience_human_touch.title: Experience and human touch
experience_human_touch.description: As technology grows, tableside service, story and community feeling differentiate restaurants from platforms.
experience_human_touch.azContext: For tourist, family and banquet formats, human touch can become a reason to choose the restaurant.
experience_human_touch.fallbackFirstStep: Give the team one personal touch rule for each table: name, signature story and a short feedback question.

sectorContext.title: 2026 sector direction
sectorContext.body: Guests expect value, convenience, digital ordering, healthier choices and human experience at the same time. A small restaurant should test the 3 most relevant directions, not chase every trend.

trendAnaliz.aiUnavailableNote: AI recommendation is currently unavailable; a general static recommendation is shown.
trendAnaliz.ahilikQuote: In the craft tradition, innovation is not chasing fashion; it is seeing customer need early and applying it with mastery.

lokasyonAnaliz.title: Location Analysis
lokasyonAnaliz.subtitle: Assess a new or existing restaurant site with franchise location criteria, breakeven sales, and risk cards.
lokasyonAnaliz.lockedTitle: Location Analysis is for KALFA members
lokasyonAnaliz.lockedBody: This tool provides a location score, breakeven sales target, and AI/fallback recommendations. KALFA access is required.

avmExtra.title: Mall extra checks
avmExtra.entryCounts: Are entrance counts and peak hours known?
avmExtra.freightLift: Is freight lift and storage distance workable?

corner_visibility.label: Corner store and visibility
corner_visibility.tooltip: Can the sign, facade, and entrance be seen from the main movement direction?

pedestrian_traffic.label: High pedestrian traffic
pedestrian_traffic.tooltip: Is there enough real foot traffic passing the point?

car_traffic.label: High car traffic
car_traffic.tooltip: Does car flow help discovery and spontaneous visits?

parking_access.label: Parking access
parking_access.tooltip: Is there real short-stop or longer parking access?

income_density.label: Middle-upper income area
income_density.tooltip: Can the surrounding audience carry your price level?

education_nearby.label: Education nearby
education_nearby.tooltip: Do universities, colleges, or learning centers create young demand?

office_residential_mix.label: Office and residential mix
office_residential_mix.tooltip: Can the point create both lunch and evening demand?

ready_parking.label: Ready parking
ready_parking.tooltip: Is there a nearby parking solution you can explain to guests?

day_evening_energy.label: Day and evening energy
day_evening_energy.tooltip: Does the location work beyond a single daypart?

wide_entry.label: Wide entry and access
wide_entry.tooltip: Is the entrance clear, open, and easy to choose?

busy_sidewalk_side.label: Busy sidewalk side
busy_sidewalk_side.tooltip: Is the point on the more-used side of the street?

low_sun_side.label: Lower sun exposure
low_sun_side.tooltip: Do sun and heat avoid hurting seating and facade comfort?

outdoor_seating.label: Outdoor seating option
outdoor_seating.tooltip: Is even a small visible outdoor or waiting area possible?

mall_escalator_position.label: Mall escalator position
mall_escalator_position.tooltip: Is the point near the densest escalator flow or right-hand side?

mall_dedicated_seating.label: Mall dedicated seating
mall_dedicated_seating.tooltip: Is there owned seating and control beyond shared foodcourt space?

breakeven.title: Breakeven point
breakeven.resultHelp: After this sales level, the site has a realistic chance to enter profit zone.
breakeven.unsustainable: With this cost structure, gross margin is too low; the site does not look sustainable.

recommendations.title: Recommendation {index}
recommendations.marginWarning: Recheck rent and variable costs; breakeven sales may be above realistic market demand.
recommendations.observeDayparts: Do not decide before observing lunch, evening, and weekend demand separately.

recommendations.parkingCheck: Simplify the guest path: show parking, entrance, and ordering directions separately in Google Business and Instagram highlights.
recommendations.rentGate: Judge rent by breakeven sales, not emotion; if the target is unrealistic, do not sign without renegotiation.

lokasyonAnaliz.ahilikQuote: In Ahilik, a good place is not only visible; it carries its cost, flow, and guest promise.

pricing.title: Marketinq Ocağı Pricing
pricing.slogan: The mark of mastery, the digital guild seal
pricing.subtitle: Choose the toolset that matches your restaurant stage: free start, KALFA management, or USTA-level analytics.

sagird.name: SAGIRD
sagird.description: For restaurants that want to start with simple calculations and core direction.

kalfa.name: KALFA
kalfa.description: For small restaurants that want data-backed decisions for sales, seasonality, ads, and operations.

usta.name: USTA
usta.description: For teams that need deeper finance, persona, and expansion analytics.

pricing.launchNote: Launch offer: all packages are free until September 1, 2026. Standard pricing applies after.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: The common questions before starting a subscription.

items.question: How do I subscribe?
items.answer: For KALFA and USTA, use the WhatsApp button and the team will send activation steps.

items.question: Can I change tiers?
items.answer: Yes. As your restaurant grows, the package can be upgraded or adjusted.

items.question: How long does the free tier last?
items.answer: SAGIRD is free for starting out and trying the core tools.

items.question: How does payment work?
items.answer: There is no online payment on this page. Payment and activation are handled over WhatsApp.

items.question: Can I cancel?
items.answer: Yes. Contact the team before the next period begins to stop the subscription.

bottomCta.title: Which package fits you?
bottomCta.body: Send your restaurant type and current stage, and we will help choose the right package.

pricing.whatsappMessage: Hello, I would like information about the {tier} package

registrationGate.title: This content is free
registrationGate.description: Just create a free account to continue — no payment required, all content and tools unlock instantly.
registrationGate.readPreview: You have read the first 40% of {title}.
registrationGate.benefit1Desc: Blog articles, news analysis, and premium details
registrationGate.benefit2Desc: Food Cost, P&L, KAZAN AI and 17 marketing tools
registrationGate.benefit3Desc: No payment required, just create an account

kazanWidget.welcome: Hello. I am **KAZAN AI**. I explain food cost, P&L, AQTA, delivery and opening decisions with numbers.
kazanWidget.prompt1: My food cost is 38%. How do I bring it down to 30-32%?
kazanWidget.prompt2: Which numbers should I look at first in a restaurant P&L report?
kazanWidget.prompt3: What should I do this week to prepare for an AQTA inspection?
kazanWidget.prompt4: How do I keep my delivery menu profitable with Wolt and Bolt Food commission?
kazanWidget.errorFallback: Live response is delayed right now. Measure the problem: write food cost, labor cost, delivery commission and sales mix separately. Then start with the relevant toolkit: [Toolkit](/toolkit).
kazanWidget.leadGateRequired: To continue the AI response, I need to save your contact details first.
kazanWidget.leadNamePhoneRequired: Enter your name and phone, then we can continue.
kazanWidget.leadSaveError: Data was not saved. Check the phone format and try again.
kazanWidget.leadActionRequired: To unlock this step, save your name and phone number first.
kazanWidget.subtitle: HoReCa decision assistant
kazanWidget.leadFormTitle: To give you a more accurate answer:
kazanWidget.leadFormDesc: Let me save your name, phone and business type, then continue with your question.
kazanWidget.inputPlaceholder: E.g.: food cost 38%, what should I do?
kazanWidget.aiDisclaimer: AI responses are for decision support purposes.
